---
title: "Set up your environment"
description: "Fork a package, then install Viash and Nextflow"
order: 10
---

## Fork and clone the repository

Each package's **source** lives in its own GitHub repository under
[openpipelines-bio](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio). These repos hold only
source code; the built packages are produced by Viash Hub (see
[Project structure](project-structure.qmd#sec-project-structure)).

| Package | Repository |
|---|---|
| `openpipeline` | [github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline) |
| `openpipeline_spatial` | [github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial) |
| `openpipeline_qc` | [github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc) |
| `openpipeline_composed` | [github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_composed](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_composed) |

:::{.callout-note appearance="simple"}
`openpipeline_rapids` is [coming soon](../guides/gpu.qmd).
:::

Pick the package you want to contribute to, create your own copy with the
**Fork** button, then clone your fork and add the original as an `upstream`
remote. Replace `<repo>` with the package name (e.g. `openpipeline_spatial`):

::: {.panel-tabset}

## HTTPS

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<YOUR USERNAME>/<repo>.git
cd <repo>
git remote add upstream https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/<repo>.git
```

## SSH

```bash
git clone git@github.com:<YOUR USERNAME>/<repo>.git
cd <repo>
git remote add upstream git@github.com:openpipelines-bio/<repo>.git
```

:::

## Install Viash and Nextflow {#sec-install-viash-nextflow}

OpenPipeline is built with [Viash](https://viash.io) and
[Nextflow](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/index.html), so you need both,
plus [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) and Java.

:::{.callout-tip}
## Follow the Viash installation guide
For detailed, up-to-date steps, follow the
[**Viash installation guide**](https://viash.io/installation/). Nextflow has its
own [installation docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/install.html).
:::

Once installed, check both work:

```bash
nextflow run hello -with-docker
viash --version
```

That's everything you need to build and test locally. You'll fetch the test
resources as part of the [local workflow](development-workflow.qmd#sec-test-resources),
just before running your first test.
